title: engine hardening: status.py DONE section crashes on dict-shaped ticket result
source: orchestrator finding 2026-07-06 (surfaced by halt-switch ticket's dict result)
prompt: engine/status.py crashes when rendering the DONE section: `TypeError: unsupported format string passed to dict.__format__`. Root cause: done_outcome() (engine/status.py:105-121) returns ticket.get('result') verbatim; when a ticket writes its outcome as a nested object (e.g. "result": {"status": "done", "notes": ...} -- the shape the halt-switch ticket and 0632Z engine-status ticket now use) `result` is a dict, and print_human() line 221 formats it with `:>8`, which dicts can't do. Fix inside done_outcome so it is robust to BOTH shapes: (a) result as a bare string (legacy hand-closed tickets), and (b) result as a dict -> unwrap result['status'] (and prefer result.get('notes') for the notes line when the ticket-level notes/verified/note are absent). Keep the existing precedence: results/<fname>.json self-report still wins over ticket-embedded result. Do NOT change the JSON output shape of --json beyond making report['done']['recent'][*]['result'] always a string. Add a one-line inline comment noting the dict-shape case. Minimal, surgical -- this is the only bug in scope.
verify: python3 engine/status.py runs clean (no traceback) and the DONE section prints a status string (e.g. 'done') for every recent ticket including any with a dict-shaped result; python3 engine/status.py --json emits done.recent[*].result as a string for all entries. Capture the before/after transcript in this ticket's result block.

verify_transcript: # BEFORE (crash reproduced against store/done/20260706T-halt-switch.json, which has a dict-shaped ticket-embedded result: {"status": "done", ...})
... DONE section renders 4 entries fine, then on the halt-switch ticket:
TypeError: unsupported format string passed to dict.__format__

# FIX applied inside done_outcome() (engine/status.py): when ticket-embedded result.get("result") is a dict, unwrap result["status"] as the status string and prefer result["notes"] for the notes line when ticket-level notes/verified/note are absent. Bare-string legacy shape and results/<fname>.json self-report precedence unchanged.
... runs clean, no traceback. DONE section shows:
- [    done]  20260706T-halt-switch.json  — ... (dict-shaped result unwrapped to "done", notes pulled from result["notes"] since ticket has no top-level notes/verified/note)
$ python3 engine/status.py --json | python3 -c "import json,sys; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print([type(x['result']).__name__ for x in d['done']['recent']])"
['str', 'str', 'str', 'str', 'str']  -- all 5 recent done entries confirmed string-typed
result.notes: Root cause was in done_outcome() fallback branch (ticket-embedded result, used when results/<fname>.json self-report is absent): it returned ticket.get("result") verbatim without checking shape. Fix adds a single isinstance(result, dict) branch with an inline comment, unwrapping status/notes. Confirmed against store/done/20260706T-halt-switch.json, the real ticket already exhibiting the dict shape. No other behavior changed; --json shape unchanged beyond guaranteeing recent[*].result is always a string.
